# Internal: Classify each base-image slot. Branches on the (ORIGIN, ANCHOR) pair:
#   ANCHOR set + HEAD 200                 → "skip"     (cache hit; locator stands).
#   ANCHOR set + HEAD 404 + ORIGIN set    → "enshrine" (stale cache; regenerate
#                                            from ORIGIN — extract overwrites the
#                                            stale value with the new locator).
#   ANCHOR set + HEAD 404 + ORIGIN empty  → fatal      (orchestration wrote a
#                                            hallmark-pin locator that should
#                                            resolve; no ORIGIN to regenerate from).
#   ANCHOR empty + ORIGIN set             → "enshrine" (fresh enshrine).
#   Both empty                            → "empty"    (omitted from build).
# Total to-enshrine count is persisted so rbfd_enshrine can decide whether to
# submit Cloud Build at all.
zrbfd_enshrine_classify_slots() {
  zrbfd_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "zrbfd_enshrine_classify_slots: token required"

  buc_step "Classifying enshrine slots (skip-if-set vs. submit)"

  local z_n=""
  local z_origin_var=""
  local z_anchor_var=""
  local z_origin=""
  local z_anchor=""
  local z_pkg_path=""
  local z_tag=""
  local z_class_file=""
  local z_status_file=""
  local z_response_file=""
  local z_stderr_file=""
  local z_http_code=""
  local z_to_enshrine_count=0

  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ORIGIN"
    z_anchor_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ANCHOR"
    z_origin="${!z_origin_var:-}"
    z_anchor="${!z_anchor_var:-}"
    z_class_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}${z_n}_classification.txt"

    if test -n "${z_anchor}"; then
      case "${z_anchor}" in
        *:*) : ;;
        *)   buc_die "Invalid ${z_anchor_var} locator format (expected package-path:tag): ${z_anchor}" ;;
      esac
      z_pkg_path="${z_anchor%:*}"
      z_tag="${z_anchor##*:}"
      test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
      test -n "${z_tag}"      || buc_die "Tag is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"

      z_status_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}${z_n}_skip_status.txt"
      z_response_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}${z_n}_skip_response.txt"
      z_stderr_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}${z_n}_skip_stderr.txt"

      curl --head -sS \
        --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
        --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}" \
        -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}" \
        -H "Accept: ${ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES}" \
        -w "%{http_code}" \
        -o "${z_response_file}" \
        "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_pkg_path}/manifests/${z_tag}" \
        > "${z_status_file}" 2>"${z_stderr_file}" \
        || buc_die "HEAD request failed for pre-populated ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor} — see ${z_stderr_file}"

      z_http_code=$(<"${z_status_file}")
      test -n "${z_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty for pre-populated ${z_anchor_var}"

      if test "${z_http_code}" = "404"; then
        # ANCHOR is a derived cache; ORIGIN is its source of truth. A 404 with
        # ORIGIN populated means the cache is stale (depot rotated, or upstream
        # digest moved) — regenerate. With ORIGIN empty, the locator was written
        # by orchestration as a hallmark-pin and 404 is a contract violation.
        if test -n "${z_origin}"; then
          printf 'enshrine' > "${z_class_file}" \
            || buc_die "Failed to write classification for slot ${z_n}"
          z_to_enshrine_count=$((z_to_enshrine_count + 1))
          buc_log_args "Slot ${z_n} stale cache (404 on ${z_anchor}) — regenerating from ORIGIN=${z_origin}"
          continue
        fi
        buc_die "Pre-populated ${z_anchor_var} not reachable in GAR: ${z_anchor} — caller wrote an unresolvable locator"
      elif test "${z_http_code}" != "200"; then
        buc_die "Unexpected HTTP ${z_http_code} for pre-populated ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
      fi

Build and tests are green; the lone fail (`rv_rbrv_all_vessels`) is the pre-existing baseline noted in BBAAz/BBAA2/BBAA0 commits.

Now for the notch.

params.intent: BBAAs enshrine-classifier-stale-cache-regenerate: zrbfd_enshrine_classify_slots conflated two qualitatively different cases when RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR was populated and HEAD returned 404 — orchestration-written hallmark-pin (ANCHOR set, ORIGIN empty) and committed-derived cache (ANCHOR set, ORIGIN set, e.g. forge vessel's rust:slim-bookworm enshrinement). BBAA0's skip-if-set design fail-fasted on both with 'caller wrote an unresolvable locator', which is correct for the pin case but wrong for the cache case: ORIGIN is the source of truth and the stale ANCHOR is a derived value that can be regenerated. Surfaced empirically in BBAAs onboarding-sequence run on a fresh canonical-established depot — the committed forge ANCHOR rbi_es/rust-slim-bookworm-caaf9ca7ac:rust-slim-bookworm-caaf9ca7ac was depot-stale (no enshrinement in the new GAR), and the classifier fatal-died before the regenerable CB path could run. Fix branches the 404 case on ORIGIN: with ORIGIN set, write 'enshrine' classification (extract step overwrites the stale value with the freshly-computed locator); with ORIGIN empty, keep the existing fatal die. Pipeline downstream of the classifier needs no changes — submit already passes ORIGIN substitutions only for to-enshrine slots, and extract already overwrites existing ANCHOR lines via in-place line replacement. Spec sweep: RBSAE head NOTE rewritten to describe the four-way semantics (skip / regenerate / fatal-pin / fresh-enshrine / empty); Classify Slots step expanded from 'pre-populated vs to-enshrine' to the explicit 404+ORIGIN-set branch; Extract step's 'pre-populated slots not touched' updated to 'skip slots not touched, stale-cache reclassifications written through'. BCG audit: test command over [[ ]], braced quoted expansion, || buc_die on every I/O, mutable locals for per-iteration synthesized values (Exception 2), comment justifying non-obvious WHY (asymmetric ORIGIN-set vs ORIGIN-empty handling on 404), continue inside for loop, no new bash 4+ features, no heredocs. Verification: bash -n green, theurge build green, 0/0 unit tests pass, fast suite 25/26 (rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA0/BBAA2/BBAAz baseline). Empirical re-run of ordain-airgap deferred per docket — operator drives next mount.

attachment.prompt: Tell me about a 'skip' slot: that sounds made up.

content.text:
Now to your message — fair callout, "skip" was a vocabulary import from the bash that I didn't think hard enough about.

## Where "skip" actually lives

In the **bash**, BBAA0 introduced a per-slot classification file at `${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}{n}_classification.txt` containing one of three literal strings: `skip`, `enshrine`, `empty`. The classifier writes one of those; `zrbfd_enshrine_submit` reads them at rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1031:

```bash
if test "${z_sub_class}" = "enshrine"; then
  ...pass ORIGIN substitution to CB...
fi
```

Only `enshrine` carries semantic load — `skip` and `empty` both mean "don't pass ORIGIN to the CB step." The classifier's docstring (pre-existing, BBAA0) already used `"skip"` as the wire-format value name.

## Where I overstepped

The **spec** (RBSAE) before my edit didn't use `skip`. It classified slots as `*pre-populated*` vs `*to-enshrine*` — state-oriented language, not action-oriented. The wire-format string `skip` and the spec term `pre-populated` were a silent mismatch since BBAA0.

My edit introduced `*Skip*` as a defined classification label in the spec, importing the bash wire-format vocabulary into the spec's prose. I should not have done this without flagging it — "skip" describes the *engine's action* on a slot, not a property of the slot itself. A populated-and-reachable slot is *what it is* whether or not enshrine ever runs; calling it a "skip slot" conflates the slot with the verb the engine applies to it.

## What I'd recommend

Revert the spec terminology to keep `*pre-populated*` (state) and `*to-enshrine*` (action description) consistent with what the spec used before, while still describing the new four-way 404+ORIGIN branch. The bash docstring's `"skip"` / `"enshrine"` / `"empty"` stays in the bash because those are the literal classification-file strings. The spec talks about slot states; the bash talks about wire-format values. Boundary respected.

Concretely, three small edits to my spec changes:
- Head NOTE: "On HEAD 200, the slot's ANCHOR is left intact" (no `*Skip*` label)
- Classify Slots: replace `*skip*` with `*pre-populated*` in the HEAD-200 sub-bullet
- Extract step: revert "Skip slots not touched" back to "Pre-populated slots not touched", with the stale-cache exception added

Want me to make these revisions and amend, or leave it as-is?

content.text:
You're right. The right move is the opposite direction.

## Which tabtarget calls the classifier

Only `rbw-dE` (DirectorEnshrinesVessel). The chain: `rbw-dE` → `rbfd_enshrine` → `zrbfd_enshrine_classify_slots` → `zrbfd_enshrine_submit` → `zrbfd_enshrine_extract_anchors`. Ordain (`rbw-fO`), build, mirror, graft, and kludge do NOT call this classifier. The classifier is enshrine-only.

## Why it isn't unconditional

It used to be. **BBAA0 overloaded enshrine to be two things at once**: per its commit message, "enshrine on the consumer becomes a skip-if-set HEAD-validation gate (no copy, no Cloud Build job)." So a single tabtarget now handles two qualitatively different jobs:

1. **Mirror upstream → GAR**: vessel has `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN` populated with an upstream tag (rust:slim-bookworm). Enshrine inspects upstream, computes content-addressed ANCHOR, copies to GAR, writes ANCHOR back. This is what `rbgje01-enshrine-copy.sh` does. Always recomputes, idempotent (skopeo copy is content-deduplicated).

2. **Validate orchestration-pinned hallmark**: airgap-bottle has `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR=rbi_hm/<HALLMARK>/...` written by orchestration just before the call. Enshrine HEAD-validates that the hallmark is reachable, leaves the slot intact. No CB job runs.

Job (1) wants unconditional replace. Job (2) wants HEAD-only no-op. The classifier exists to switch between them based on whether ANCHOR is populated.

## Why my fix is wrong-way-round

I added a third sub-case to the classifier (404 + ORIGIN-set → regenerate). That's adding more branching to a switch that exists because of an overload. The right move is to **remove the overload**, not refine its switch:

- Make `rbfd_enshrine` always run the CB job for slots with `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN` populated. No classifier. No HEAD-validation step. The CB script's `rbgje01-enshrine-copy.sh:45` already does `test -n "${ORIGIN}" || continue` — empty-ORIGIN slots are naturally skipped at CB time. Extract writes back unconditionally. Whatever was committed in ANCHOR gets overwritten — exactly the "enshrine + commit changes RBRV to the right value" property you described.
- Remove the orchestration's "enshrine-airgap-validate" call at `rbtdro_onboarding.rs:868`. The airgap-bottle's hallmark-pin ANCHOR is written by orchestration after `ordain-forge` *succeeds and returns the hallmark* — the hallmark's existence in GAR is established by ordain's success, not by a separate validation step. The call is checking what's already known.
- Spec change is a partial revert of BBAA0's "Classify Slots" and "skip-if-set" prose: drop the classifier section, simplify the head NOTE.

Job (1) and job (2) get cleanly separated: enshrine is mirror-only; orchestration-pin needs no enshrine call.

## What I'd like to do

Revert my BBAAs notch (`f365fe89`) — it added the wrong-direction patch. Then implement the simplification above as a fresh notch. This is closer to a partial-revert of BBAA0 than to a refinement, so it's a bigger change in lines but a smaller change in concept count.

Authorize the revert + simplification, or do you want me to inspect anything else first?

  is written by orchestration after ordain-forge succeeds and returns the hallmark — the hallmark's existence in GAR is established
  by ordain's success, not by a separate validation step. The call is checking what's already known.
  - Spec change is a partial revert of BBAA0's "Classify Slots" and "skip-if-set" prose: drop the classifier section, simplify the
  head NOTE.

...looks more on target.  What was the head check about, anyway?

content.text:
There are actually **two** HEAD-validation paths in the bash. Worth distinguishing.

## (1) The classifier HEAD check — `zrbfd_enshrine_classify_slots`

This is the one I just patched and the one the simplification removes. **Pre-BBAA0 it didn't exist** — enshrine always ran the CB job. BBAA0 added it as part of the dual-purpose overload, serving two purposes:

- **Optimization**: if a vessel's `ANCHOR` is already reachable in GAR, skip the CB submission. Saves ~1-3 min of Cloud Build wall-clock per call. `skopeo copy --all` is content-deduplicated so re-running is cheap on the wire, but CB still spins up the step container, fetches the reliquary skopeo image, runs the script, and tears down — that's the cost the optimization dodges.
- **Validation gate** for the airgap-bottle's hallmark-pin: the only reason the call exists at all in the orchestration is to HEAD-check `rbi_hm/<FORGE_HALLMARK>/...`. There's no upstream to mirror; the HEAD check IS the whole job.

Once we remove the orchestration's hallmark-pin call (purpose 2 disappears), only purpose 1 remains. The optimization is small in absolute terms, irrelevant on fresh canonical depots (every slot is a cache miss anyway), and the cost it saves is dwarfed by the conjure builds it precedes (~15-20 min). Not worth the classifier complexity. Drop it with the simplification.

## (2) The ordain preflight HEAD check — `zrbfd_registry_preflight` Layer 2

Different beast, **stays untouched**. This one runs inside `rbfd_ordain` (and `rbfd_build`), not enshrine. Its job: **fast-fail before launching the conjure Cloud Build job** if a base image's locator doesn't resolve in GAR. The error tells the operator exactly what to do (run enshrine, then re-run ordain — copy-paste tabtargets in the failure message).

Without it, the operator would discover the missing base image only when CB fails minutes later with a Docker pull error. The local HEAD check turns minutes-later into seconds-now.

This preflight has been in the codebase since well before BBAA0 and serves a clean single purpose. The simplification doesn't touch it.

## Summary

| Where | Purpose | Keep? |
|---|---|---|
| `zrbfd_enshrine_classify_slots` (in `rbfd_enshrine`) | BBAA0 dual-purpose overload — optimization + hallmark-pin gate | **Remove** |
| `zrbfd_registry_preflight` Layer 2 (in `rbfd_ordain`/`rbfd_build`) | Fast-fail before CB launches if base image missing | **Keep** |

So when I do the simplification: revert the classifier whole; leave the ordain-side preflight alone. Want me to proceed with the revert + simplification on this basis?

content.text:
You're absolutely right, and that reframing kills my "optimization" argument. Let me restate.

## Enshrine's actual contract

Enshrine's job is **to compute and install the current correct ANCHOR for the current upstream**. The ANCHOR is a content-addressed locator: `<sanitized-origin>-<first10-of-current-upstream-manifest-sha256>`. The function is `(upstream tag at this moment) → (anchor)`. Calling enshrine = "I want the freshly-computed anchor for whatever upstream `rust:slim-bookworm` points to *right now*."

Re-running enshrine on the same vessel can produce a different ANCHOR — exactly when it should:

- Same depot, same upstream digest → same ANCHOR → no-op (correctly).
- Same depot, upstream digest moved → **different ANCHOR** → mirror the new upstream and rewrite the locator.
- New depot → new GAR; recompute and mirror.

The "upstream digest moved" case is the load-bearing one. Brad's commit `14242011` (pace `A_AAS`) records this happening empirically: "re-enshrined rust:slim-bookworm... anchor 5ae2d2ef98 → caaf9ca7ac — upstream digest moved since the prior enshrine 2026-04-04." Enshrine is supposed to surface that change, not hide it.

## What "skip-if-set HEAD-validate" actually does

`HEAD 200` on the cached ANCHOR proves *some* manifest exists at that locator. It does **not** prove that manifest matches the current upstream. The cache could be a month old, pointing at a stale version of `rust:slim-bookworm`, and HEAD-200 would succeed because the *old* enshrinement is still in GAR.

So the classifier's HEAD-200 path is silently locking the vessel to the cached digest forever, no matter how far upstream drifts. Subsequent `rbw-dE` invocations on that vessel become permanent no-ops. The "did upstream move?" question — the only question enshrine exists to answer — never gets asked.

That's not an optimization. That's an active break of the contract. The 1-3 min CB wall-clock is exactly what's *supposed* to be paid: it's the cost of the inspect+digest computation that determines whether the cache is still right.

## Confirmation of direction

Simplification stands, with the framing corrected:

- `rbfd_enshrine` always runs the CB job for slots with `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN` populated. CB inspects current upstream, computes current digest, copies, returns the locator. Extract overwrites whatever was committed in `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR` with the just-computed value. **Re-runs are idempotent w.r.t. upstream state, not w.r.t. the file's prior contents.** That distinction was BBAA0's mistake.
- Orchestration's `enshrine-airgap-validate` call goes away. Hallmark-pin existence is established by ordain-forge's success.
- Spec partial-revert of BBAA0 prose: the classifier section disappears; head NOTE drops "skip-if-set" and reads as "enshrine recomputes the locator from current upstream every call; the prior committed ANCHOR is overwritten."

Same code change as before, but with honest reasoning. Authorize and I'll revert `f365fe89` and notch the simplification.

f365fe89 jjb:1011-7cb6a2c6:₢BBAAs:n: BBAAs enshrine-classifier-stale-cache-regenerate: zrbfd_enshrine_classify_slots conflated two qualitatively different cases when RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR was populated and HEAD returned 404 — orchestration-written hallmark-pin (ANCHOR set, ORIGIN empty) and committed-derived cache (ANCHOR set, ORIGIN set, e.g. forge vessel's rust:slim-bookworm enshrinement). BBAA0's skip-if-set design fail-fasted on both with 'caller wrote an unresolvable locator', which is correct for the pin case but wrong for the cache case: ORIGIN is the source of truth and the stale ANCHOR is a derived value that can be regenerated. Surfaced empirically in BBAAs onboarding-sequence run on a fresh canonical-established depot — the committed forge ANCHOR rbi_es/rust-slim-bookworm-caaf9ca7ac:rust-slim-bookworm-caaf9ca7ac was depot-stale (no enshrinement in the new GAR), and the classifier fatal-died before the regenerable CB path could run. Fix branches the 404 case on ORIGIN: with ORIGIN set, write 'enshrine' classification (extract step overwrites the stale value with the freshly-computed locator); with ORIGIN empty, keep the existing fatal die. Pipeline downstream of the classifier needs no changes — submit already passes ORIGIN substitutions only for to-enshrine slots, and extract already overwrites existing ANCHOR lines via in-place line replacement. Spec sweep: RBSAE head NOTE rewritten to describe the four-way semantics (skip / regenerate / fatal-pin / fresh-enshrine / empty); Classify Slots step expanded from 'pre-populated vs to-enshrine' to the explicit 404+ORIGIN-set branch; Extract step's 'pre-populated slots not touched' updated to 'skip slots not touched, stale-cache reclassifications written through'. content.content:
# Internal: Classify each base-image slot for skip-if-set semantics.
#   Slot with ANCHOR set     → HEAD-validate the locator in GAR, write "skip".
#   Slot with ORIGIN only    → write "enshrine" (submit to CB).
#   Slot with neither set    → write "empty" (omitted from build).
# A 404 on a pre-populated ANCHOR is fatal — the caller wrote an unreachable
# locator. Total to-enshrine count is persisted so rbfd_enshrine can decide
# whether to submit Cloud Build at all.
zrbfd_enshrine_classify_slots() {
  zrbfd_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "zrbfd_enshrine_classify_slots: token required"

  buc_step "Classifying enshrine slots (skip-if-set vs. submit)"

  local z_n=""
  local z_origin_var=""
  local z_anchor_var=""
  local z_origin=""
  local z_anchor=""
  local z_pkg_path=""
  local z_tag=""
  local z_class_file=""
  local z_status_file=""
  local z_response_file=""
  local z_stderr_file=""
  local z_http_code=""
  local z_to_enshrine_count=0

  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ORIGIN"
    z_anchor_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ANCHOR"
    z_origin="${!z_origin_var:-}"
    z_anchor="${!z_anchor_var:-}"
    z_class_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}${z_n}_classification.txt"

    if test -n "${z_anchor}"; then
      case "${z_anchor}" in
        *:*) : ;;
        *)   buc_die "Invalid ${z_anchor_var} locator format (expected package-path:tag): ${z_anchor}" ;;
      esac
      z_pkg_path="${z_anchor%:*}"
      z_tag="${z_anchor##*:}"
      test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
      test -n "${z_tag}"      || buc_die "Tag is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"

      z_status_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}${z_n}_skip_status.txt"
      z_response_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}${z_n}_skip_response.txt"
      z_stderr_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}${z_n}_skip_stderr.txt"

      curl --head -sS \
        --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
        --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}" \
        -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}" \
        -H "Accept: ${ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES}" \
        -w "%{http_code}" \
        -o "${z_response_file}" \
        "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_pkg_path}/manifests/${z_tag}" \
        > "${z_status_file}" 2>"${z_stderr_file}" \
        || buc_die "HEAD request failed for pre-populated ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor} — see ${z_stderr_file}"

      z_http_code=$(<"${z_status_file}")
      test -n "${z_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty for pre-populated ${z_anchor_var}"

      if test "${z_http_code}" = "404"; then
        buc_die "Pre-populated ${z_anchor_var} not reachable in GAR: ${z_anchor} — caller wrote an unresolvable locator"
      elif test "${z_http_code}" != "200"; then
        buc_die "Unexpected HTTP ${z_http_code} for pre-populated ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
      fi

      printf 'skip' > "${z_class_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write classification for slot ${z_n}"
      buc_info "Slot ${z_n} pre-populated and reachable: ${z_anchor}"
    elif test -n "${z_origin}"; then
      printf 'enshrine' > "${z_class_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write classification for slot ${z_n}"
      z_to_enshrine_count=$((z_to_enshrine_count + 1))
      buc_log_args "Slot ${z_n} to enshrine: ORIGIN=${z_origin}"
    else
      printf 'empty' > "${z_class_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write classification for slot ${z_n}"
    fi
  done

  printf '%s' "${z_to_enshrine_count}" > "${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}to_enshrine_count.txt" \
    || buc_die "Failed to persist to-enshrine count"
}

# Internal: Submit enshrine Cloud Build job
# Single step: skopeo inspect + copy for each ORIGIN slot, returning anchors via buildStepOutputs.
zrbfd_enshrine_submit() {
  zrbfd_sentinel

  local -r z_token="$1"

  local z_stderr_file=""
  local z_http_code=""
  local z_to_enshrine_count=0

  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ORIGIN"
    z_anchor_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ANCHOR"
    z_origin="${!z_origin_var:-}"
    z_anchor="${!z_anchor_var:-}"
    z_class_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}${z_n}_classification.txt"

    if test -n "${z_anchor}"; then
      case "${z_anchor}" in
        *:*) : ;;
        *)   buc_die "Invalid ${z_anchor_var} locator format (expected package-path:tag): ${z_anchor}" ;;
      esac
      z_pkg_path="${z_anchor%:*}"
      z_tag="${z_anchor##*:}"
      test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
      test -n "${z_tag}"      || buc_die "Tag is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"

      z_status_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}${z_n}_skip_status.txt"
      z_response_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}${z_n}_skip_response.txt"
      z_stderr_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}${z_n}_skip_stderr.txt"

      curl --head -sS \
        --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
        --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}" \
        -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}" \
        -H "Accept: ${ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES}" \
        -w "%{http_code}" \
        -o "${z_response_file}" \
        "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_pkg_path}/manifests/${z_tag}" \
        > "${z_status_file}" 2>"${z_stderr_file}" \
        || buc_die "HEAD request failed for pre-populated ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor} — see ${z_stderr_file}"

      z_http_code=$(<"${z_status_file}")
      test -n "${z_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty for pre-populated ${z_anchor_var}"

      if test "${z_http_code}" = "404"; then
        buc_die "Pre-populated ${z_anchor_var} not reachable in GAR: ${z_anchor} — caller wrote an unresolvable locator"
      elif test "${z_http_code}" != "200"; then
        buc_die "Unexpected HTTP ${z_http_code} for pre-populated ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
      fi

      printf 'skip' > "${z_class_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write classification for slot ${z_n}"
      buc_info "Slot ${z_n} pre-populated and reachable: ${z_anchor}"
    elif test -n "${z_origin}"; then
      printf 'enshrine' > "${z_class_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write classification for slot ${z_n}"
      z_to_enshrine_count=$((z_to_enshrine_count + 1))
      buc_log_args "Slot ${z_n} to enshrine: ORIGIN=${z_origin}"
    else
      printf 'empty' > "${z_class_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write classification for slot ${z_n}"
    fi
  done

  printf '%s' "${z_to_enshrine_count}" > "${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}to_enshrine_count.txt" \
    || buc_die "Failed to persist to-enshrine count"
}

# Internal: Submit enshrine Cloud Build job
# Single step: skopeo inspect + copy for each ORIGIN slot, returning anchors via buildStepOutputs.
zrbfd_enshrine_submit() {
  zrbfd_sentinel

  local -r z_token="$1"

params.intent: BBAAs revert f365fe89 (enshrine-classifier-stale-cache-regenerate). The four-way classifier patch added more branching to a switch that exists only because BBAA0 overloaded enshrine to be both 'mirror upstream + write back' (forge case) and 'HEAD-validate orchestration-pinned hallmark' (airgap-bottle case). Refining the switch is wrong-direction; the right move is to remove the overload. Operator-driven reframing: the classifier's HEAD-200 'skip' path is not an optimization — it actively breaks enshrine's contract. Enshrine's job is to compute the current correct ANCHOR for the current upstream; HEAD-200 on a cached locator proves only that some manifest exists at that path, not that it matches current upstream. Skipping based on cache reachability silently locks the vessel to a stale digest forever, hiding the upstream-rotated case (the case Brad's commit 14242011 records empirically: 5ae2d2ef98 → caaf9ca7ac when upstream digest moved). The next notch implements the simplification: drop the entire classifier, make rbfd_enshrine always submit the CB job, remove the orchestration's enshrine-airgap-validate call (the hallmark-pin's existence is established by ordain-forge's success, not by a separate validation step), partial-revert BBAA0 spec prose accordingly. This notch restores rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh and RBSAE-ark_enshrine.adoc to their pre-f365fe89 state via git revert --no-commit. bash -n green; theurge build green (no Rust changes).

[[rbrv_image_origin]]
//axvr_variable axd_optional rbst_image_ref
{rbrv_image_origin}::
Base image declaration for
{rbrv_regime}
conjuring vessels.
Two complementary roles depending on whether the slot's
{rbrv_image_anchor}
is set:
+
- *When the slot is open (no ANCHOR yet)*: ORIGIN is the upstream registry tag the vessel author intends to mirror (e.g., `python:3.11-slim`). {rbtgo_ark_enshrine} consumes it as the source for `skopeo inspect`/`copy`.
- *When the slot is pre-populated with a hallmark-base ANCHOR*: ORIGIN is informational — a lineage marker (typically the producing vessel's sigil, e.g., `rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge`). {rbtgo_ark_enshrine} ignores ORIGIN under skip-if-set; conjure resolves the base from
{rbrv_image_anchor}.
+
The Dockerfile uses `ARG RBF_IMAGE_n` / `FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_n}` -- the Foundry substitutes the resolved reference via `--build-arg` at conjure time.

[[rbrv_image_anchor]]
//axvr_variable axd_optional rbst_locator
{rbrv_image_anchor}::
{rbst_locator}
naming a GAR-resident base image for
{rbrv_regime}
conjuring vessels.
The locator's namespace identifies how the slot was populated:
+
- `{gar_enshrines_namespace}/«ANCHOR»:«ANCHOR»` — written by
{rbtgo_ark_enshrine}.
The anchor itself is `«sanitized-origin»-«10-char-sha256»` (e.g., `python-3.11-slim-abc123def4`); the locator embeds it as both path segment and tag.
Enshrined images are stored as peer packages under the shared `{gar_enshrines_namespace}/` namespace, not per-vessel — vessels sharing the same base image origin share one enshrined artifact.
- `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/«BASENAME»:«HALLMARK»` — written by an orchestrating procedure (handbook step or onboarding-sequence fixture) when the vessel's base image is an internal hallmark (e.g., the airgap-bottle building FROM the just-ordained forge image).
The locator points at the specific
{rbtga_ark_image}
inside the producer hallmark's subtree.
{rbtgo_ark_enshrine}
treats such pre-populated slots under skip-if-set semantics — HEAD-validates and leaves the slot intact.
+
When present, conjure resolves the base image from GAR via this locator (parsing path and tag, prepending {rbrr_cloud_prefix} at use-site per the {rbst_locator} convention).
When absent, conjure passes
{rbrv_image_origin}
through as the upstream tag (development path).

{rbrv_group_grafting}::

{rbrv_regime}.

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Regime Vessel - Bottle Ifrit (Airgapped Conjure)

# Core Identity
RBRV_SIGIL=rbev-bottle-ifrit-airgap
RBRV_DESCRIPTION="Rust ifrit attack binary with network tools and echo service (airgapped)"
RBRV_USER=ifrit

# Vessel Mode
RBRV_VESSEL_MODE=conjure

# Egress Mode
RBRV_EGRESS_MODE=airgap

# Tool Image Reliquary
RBRV_RELIQUARY=r260430070211

# Conjuring Configuration — airgap Dockerfile in shared context, builds FROM enshrined forge
RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/common-ifrit-context/Dockerfile.airgap"
RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/common-ifrit-context"
RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS="linux/amd64 linux/arm64"

# Base Images (ARG/FROM pattern — IMAGE_1 is the forge image inside its hallmark subtree)
# ORIGIN names the producer vessel (lineage); ANCHOR carries the locator
# pointing at the just-ordained forge image:
#   rbi_hm/<FORGE_HALLMARK>/image:<FORGE_HALLMARK>
# Operator (handbook) or orchestration (onboarding-sequence) writes ANCHOR

# Check at least one slot is declared (ORIGIN or pre-populated ANCHOR)
  local z_has_slot=false
  local z_n=""
  local z_origin_var=""
  local z_anchor_var=""
  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ORIGIN"
    z_anchor_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ANCHOR"
    test -z "${!z_origin_var:-}" || z_has_slot=true
    test -z "${!z_anchor_var:-}" || z_has_slot=true
  done
  test "${z_has_slot}" = "true" \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' declares no base-image slots (RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN/ANCHOR)"

  # Resolve tool images from reliquary (enshrine uses skopeo from reliquary)
  zrbfc_resolve_tool_images

  # Authenticate as Director
  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  local z_token=""
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"

  # Verify reliquary tool images exist before submitting
  zrbfd_preflight_reliquary "${z_token}" "${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Per-slot classification: pre-populated ANCHOR slots are HEAD-validated and
  # left intact (skip-if-set); slots with ORIGIN-only are submitted to Cloud Build.
  # Output: ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX{1,2,3}_classification.txt holding "skip" or "enshrine".
  zrbfd_enshrine_classify_slots "${z_token}"

  local z_to_enshrine_count
  z_to_enshrine_count=$(<"${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}to_enshrine_count.txt")
  test -n "${z_to_enshrine_count}" || buc_die "Empty to-enshrine count"

  if test "${z_to_enshrine_count}" = "0"; then
    buc_info "All declared slots pre-populated — skipping Cloud Build submission"
  else
    # Submit enshrine as a Cloud Build job (skopeo runs on GCB, not locally)
    zrbfd_enshrine_submit "${z_token}"

    # Extract anchor results from build step outputs (only for to-enshrine slots)
    local -r z_rbrv_file="${z_vessel_dir}/rbrv.env"
    zrbfd_enshrine_extract_anchors "${z_rbrv_file}"
  fi

  buc_success "Enshrine complete for vessel: ${RBRV_SIGIL}"
}

  # ORIGIN by this operation; a vessel with only ANCHOR populated has nothing

  local z_has_origin=false

  done
  test "${z_has_origin}" = "true" \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' declares no upstream base-image slots (RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN)"

  zrbfc_resolve_tool_images

  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  local z_token=""
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"

  # Verify reliquary tool images exist before submitting
  zrbfd_preflight_reliquary "${z_token}" "${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Always submit the Cloud Build job. The CB step inspects the current upstream
  # for each non-empty ORIGIN slot, computes the content-addressed locator from
  # the live manifest digest, and copies to GAR. Extract overwrites whatever was
  # previously committed in RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR with the just-computed value —
  # any prior committed value is treated as a derived artifact, not a pin.
  zrbfd_enshrine_submit "${z_token}"

  local -r z_rbrv_file="${z_vessel_dir}/rbrv.env"
  zrbfd_enshrine_extract_anchors "${z_rbrv_file}"

  buc_success "Enshrine complete for vessel: ${RBRV_SIGIL}"
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (rbfd_*)

rbfd_enshrine() {
  zrbfd_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Enshrine upstream base images to GAR via Cloud Build"
  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path) and load
  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${1:-}"
  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"
  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"
  test "${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE:-}" = "conjure" \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' is not a conjure vessel (mode: ${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE:-unset})"

  # Check at least one slot is declared (ORIGIN or pre-populated ANCHOR)
  local z_has_slot=false
  local z_n=""
  local z_origin_var=""
  local z_anchor_var=""
  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ORIGIN"
    z_anchor_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ANCHOR"
    test -z "${!z_origin_var:-}" || z_has_slot=true
    test -z "${!z_anchor_var:-}" || z_has_slot=true
  done
  test "${z_has_slot}" = "true" \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' declares no base-image slots (RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN/ANCHOR)"

  # Resolve tool images from reliquary (enshrine uses skopeo from reliquary)
  zrbfc_resolve_tool_images

  # Authenticate as Director
  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  local z_token=""
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"

  # Verify reliquary tool images exist before submitting
  zrbfd_preflight_reliquary "${z_token}" "${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Per-slot classification: pre-populated ANCHOR slots are HEAD-validated and
  # left intact (skip-if-set); slots with ORIGIN-only are submitted to Cloud Build.
  # Output: ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX{1,2,3}_classification.txt holding "skip" or "enshrine".
  zrbfd_enshrine_classify_slots "${z_token}"

  local z_to_enshrine_count
  z_to_enshrine_count=$(<"${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}to_enshrine_count.txt")
  test -n "${z_to_enshrine_count}" || buc_die "Empty to-enshrine count"

  if test "${z_to_enshrine_count}" = "0"; then
    buc_info "All declared slots pre-populated — skipping Cloud Build submission"
  else
    # Submit enshrine as a Cloud Build job (skopeo runs on GCB, not locally)
    zrbfd_enshrine_submit "${z_token}"

    # Extract anchor results from build step outputs (only for to-enshrine slots)
    local -r z_rbrv_file="${z_vessel_dir}/rbrv.env"
    zrbfd_enshrine_extract_anchors "${z_rbrv_file}"
  fi

  buc_success "Enshrine complete for vessel: ${RBRV_SIGIL}"
}

# Internal: Classify each base-image slot for skip-if-set semantics.
#   Slot with ANCHOR set     → HEAD-validate the locator in GAR, write "skip".
#   Slot with ORIGIN only    → write "enshrine" (submit to CB).
#   Slot with neither set    → write "empty" (omitted from build).
# A 404 on a pre-populated ANCHOR is fatal — the caller wrote an unreachable

  # Output: ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX{1,2,3}_classification.txt holding "skip" or "enshrine".
  zrbfd_enshrine_classify_slots "${z_token}"

  local z_to_enshrine_count
  z_to_enshrine_count=$(<"${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}to_enshrine_count.txt")
  test -n "${z_to_enshrine_count}" || buc_die "Empty to-enshrine count"

  if test "${z_to_enshrine_count}" = "0"; then
    buc_info "All declared slots pre-populated — skipping Cloud Build submission"
  else
    # Submit enshrine as a Cloud Build job (skopeo runs on GCB, not locally)
    zrbfd_enshrine_submit "${z_token}"

    # Extract anchor results from build step outputs (only for to-enshrine slots)
    local -r z_rbrv_file="${z_vessel_dir}/rbrv.env"
    zrbfd_enshrine_extract_anchors "${z_rbrv_file}"
  fi

  buc_success "Enshrine complete for vessel: ${RBRV_SIGIL}"
}
toolUseResult.newString:
# Check at least one slot declares an upstream ORIGIN. ANCHOR is a derived
  # value computed by this operation from ORIGIN; a vessel with no ORIGIN has
  # nothing for enshrine to mirror.
  local z_has_origin=false
  local z_n=""
  local z_origin_var=""
  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ORIGIN"
    test -z "${!z_origin_var:-}" || z_has_origin=true
  done
  test "${z_has_origin}" = "true" \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' declares no upstream base-image slots (RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN)"

  # Resolve tool images from reliquary (enshrine uses skopeo from reliquary)
  zrbfc_resolve_tool_images

  # Authenticate as Director
  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  local z_token=""
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"

  # Verify reliquary tool images exist before submitting
  zrbfd_preflight_reliquary "${z_token}" "${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Always submit the Cloud Build job. The CB step inspects current upstream
  # for each non-empty ORIGIN slot, computes the content-addressed locator
  # from the live manifest digest, and copies into GAR. Extract overwrites
  # whatever was previously committed in RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR with the just-
  # computed value — the ANCHOR is a derived artifact of the current upstream,
  # not a pin.
  zrbfd_enshrine_submit "${z_token}"

  local -r z_rbrv_file="${z_vessel_dir}/rbrv.env"
  zrbfd_enshrine_extract_anchors "${z_rbrv_file}"

  buc_success "Enshrine complete for vessel: ${RBRV_SIGIL}"
}

zrbfd_enshrine_extract_anchors "${z_rbrv_file}"

  buc_success "Enshrine complete for vessel: ${RBRV_SIGIL}"
}

# Internal: Classify each base-image slot for skip-if-set semantics.
#   Slot with ANCHOR set     → HEAD-validate the locator in GAR, write "skip".
#   Slot with ORIGIN only    → write "enshrine" (submit to CB).
#   Slot with neither set    → write "empty" (omitted from build).
# A 404 on a pre-populated ANCHOR is fatal — the caller wrote an unreachable
# locator. Total to-enshrine count is persisted so rbfd_enshrine can decide
# whether to submit Cloud Build at all.
zrbfd_enshrine_classify_slots() {
  zrbfd_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "zrbfd_enshrine_classify_slots: token required"

  buc_step "Classifying enshrine slots (skip-if-set vs. submit)"

  local z_n=""
  local z_origin_var=""
  local z_anchor_var=""
  local z_origin=""
  local z_anchor=""
  local z_pkg_path=""
  local z_tag=""
  local z_class_file=""
  local z_status_file=""
  local z_response_file=""
  local z_stderr_file=""
  local z_http_code=""
  local z_to_enshrine_count=0

  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ORIGIN"
    z_anchor_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ANCHOR"
    z_origin="${!z_origin_var:-}"
    z_anchor="${!z_anchor_var:-}"
    z_class_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}${z_n}_classification.txt"

    if test -n "${z_anchor}"; then
      case "${z_anchor}" in
        *:*) : ;;
        *)   buc_die "Invalid ${z_anchor_var} locator format (expected package-path:tag): ${z_anchor}" ;;
      esac
      z_pkg_path="${z_anchor%:*}"
      z_tag="${z_anchor##*:}"
      test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
      test -n "${z_tag}"      || buc_die "Tag is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"

      z_status_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}${z_n}_skip_status.txt"
      z_response_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}${z_n}_skip_response.txt"
      z_stderr_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}${z_n}_skip_stderr.txt"

      curl --head -sS \
        --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
        --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}" \
        -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}" \
        -H "Accept: ${ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES}" \
        -w "%{http_code}" \
        -o "${z_response_file}" \
        "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_pkg_path}/manifests/${z_tag}" \
        > "${z_status_file}" 2>"${z_stderr_file}" \
        || buc_die "HEAD request failed for pre-populated ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor} — see ${z_stderr_file}"

      z_http_code=$(<"${z_status_file}")
      test -n "${z_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty for pre-populated ${z_anchor_var}"

      if test "${z_http_code}" = "404"; then
        buc_die "Pre-populated ${z_anchor_var} not reachable in GAR: ${z_anchor} — caller wrote an unresolvable locator"
      elif test "${z_http_code}" != "200"; then
        buc_die "Unexpected HTTP ${z_http_code} for pre-populated ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
      fi

      printf 'skip' > "${z_class_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write classification for slot ${z_n}"
      buc_info "Slot ${z_n} pre-populated and reachable: ${z_anchor}"
    elif test -n "${z_origin}"; then
      printf 'enshrine' > "${z_class_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write classification for slot ${z_n}"
      z_to_enshrine_count=$((z_to_enshrine_count + 1))
      buc_log_args "Slot ${z_n} to enshrine: ORIGIN=${z_origin}"
    else
      printf 'empty' > "${z_class_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write classification for slot ${z_n}"
    fi
  done

  printf '%s' "${z_to_enshrine_count}" > "${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}to_enshrine_count.txt" \
    || buc_die "Failed to persist to-enshrine count"
}

# Internal: Submit enshrine Cloud Build job
# Single step: skopeo inspect + copy for each ORIGIN slot, returning anchors via buildStepOutputs.
zrbfd_enshrine_submit() {
  zrbfd_sentinel

  local -r z_token="$1"

  buc_step "Constructing enshrine Cloud Build resource"
  local -r z_gar_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
  local -r z_gar_path="${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
  local -r z_mason_sa="projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/serviceAccounts/${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}"

  # Assemble enshrine step from script
  local -r z_script_path="${ZRBFD_RBGJE_STEPS_DIR}/rbgje01-enshrine-copy.sh"
  test -f "${z_script_path}" || buc_die "Enshrine step script not found: ${z_script_path}"

  local -r z_body_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}body.txt"
  local -r z_escaped_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}escaped.txt"

  buc_log_args "Reading enshrine step script (skip shebang)"
  tail -n +2 "${z_script_path}" > "${z_body_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to read enshrine step script"
  local z_body=""
  z_body=$(<"${z_body_file}")
  test -n "${z_body}" || buc_die "Empty enshrine script body"

  printf '#!/bin/bash\n%s' "${z_body}" > "${z_escaped_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to write escaped enshrine script body"

  local -r z_step_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}step.json"
  echo "[]" > "${z_step_file}" || buc_die "Failed to initialize enshrine step JSON"

  local -r z_step_built="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}step_built.json"
  jq \
    --arg name "${z_rbfc_tool_skopeo}" \
    --arg id "enshrine-copy" \
    --rawfile script "${z_escaped_file}" \
    '. + [{name: $name, id: $id, script: $script}]' \
    "${z_step_file}" > "${z_step_built}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to build enshrine step JSON"
  mv "${z_step_built}" "${z_step_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to finalize enshrine step JSON"

  # Compose Build resource JSON
  buc_log_args "Composing enshrine Build resource JSON"
  local -r z_build_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}build.json"

  # ORIGIN substitution per slot: pass through only when the slot is classified
  # "enshrine"; "skip" (pre-populated ANCHOR) and "empty" map to empty string so
  # the Cloud Build step omits them.
  local z_sub_n=""
  local z_sub_class_file=""
  local z_sub_class=""
  local z_sub_origin_var=""
  local z_sub_origin_1=""
  local z_sub_origin_2=""
  local z_sub_origin_3=""
  for z_sub_n in 1 2 3; do
    z_sub_class_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}${z_sub_n}_classification.txt"
    z_sub_class=$(<"${z_sub_class_file}")
    z_sub_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_sub_n}_ORIGIN"
    if test "${z_sub_class}" = "enshrine"; then
      case "${z_sub_n}" in
        1) z_sub_origin_1="${!z_sub_origin_var:-}" ;;
        2) z_sub_origin_2="${!z_sub_origin_var:-}" ;;
        3) z_sub_origin_3="${!z_sub_origin_var:-}" ;;
      esac
    fi
  done

  jq -n \
    --slurpfile zjq_steps        "${z_step_file}" \
    --arg zjq_sa                 "${z_mason_sa}" \
    --arg zjq_gar_host           "${z_gar_host}" \
    --arg zjq_gar_path           "${z_gar_path}" \
    --arg zjq_enshrines_root     "${RBGL_ENSHRINES_ROOT}" \
    --arg zjq_enshrines_category "${RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_ENSHRINES}" \
    --arg zjq_origin_1           "${z_sub_origin_1}" \
    --arg zjq_origin_2           "${z_sub_origin_2}" \
    --arg zjq_origin_3           "${z_sub_origin_3}" \

    z_origin="${!z_origin_var:-}"
    z_anchor="${!z_anchor_var:-}"
    z_class_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}${z_n}_classification.txt"

    if test -n "${z_anchor}"; then
      case "${z_anchor}" in
        *:*) : ;;
        *)   buc_die "Invalid ${z_anchor_var} locator format (expected package-path:tag): ${z_anchor}" ;;
      esac
      z_pkg_path="${z_anchor%:*}"
      z_tag="${z_anchor##*:}"
      test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
      test -n "${z_tag}"      || buc_die "Tag is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"

      z_status_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}${z_n}_skip_status.txt"
      z_response_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}${z_n}_skip_response.txt"
      z_stderr_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}${z_n}_skip_stderr.txt"

      curl --head -sS \
        --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
        --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}" \
        -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}" \
        -H "Accept: ${ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES}" \
        -w "%{http_code}" \
        -o "${z_response_file}" \
        "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_pkg_path}/manifests/${z_tag}" \
        > "${z_status_file}" 2>"${z_stderr_file}" \
        || buc_die "HEAD request failed for pre-populated ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor} — see ${z_stderr_file}"

      z_http_code=$(<"${z_status_file}")
      test -n "${z_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty for pre-populated ${z_anchor_var}"

      if test "${z_http_code}" = "404"; then
        buc_die "Pre-populated ${z_anchor_var} not reachable in GAR: ${z_anchor} — caller wrote an unresolvable locator"
      elif test "${z_http_code}" != "200"; then
        buc_die "Unexpected HTTP ${z_http_code} for pre-populated ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
      fi

      printf 'skip' > "${z_class_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write classification for slot ${z_n}"
      buc_info "Slot ${z_n} pre-populated and reachable: ${z_anchor}"
    elif test -n "${z_origin}"; then
      printf 'enshrine' > "${z_class_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write classification for slot ${z_n}"
      z_to_enshrine_count=$((z_to_enshrine_count + 1))
      buc_log_args "Slot ${z_n} to enshrine: ORIGIN=${z_origin}"
    else
      printf 'empty' > "${z_class_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write classification for slot ${z_n}"
    fi
  done

  printf '%s' "${z_to_enshrine_count}" > "${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}to_enshrine_count.txt" \
    || buc_die "Failed to persist to-enshrine count"
}

input.old_string:
# Internal: Classify each base-image slot for skip-if-set semantics.
#   Slot with ANCHOR set     → HEAD-validate the locator in GAR, write "skip".
#   Slot with ORIGIN only    → write "enshrine" (submit to CB).
#   Slot with neither set    → write "empty" (omitted from build).
# A 404 on a pre-populated ANCHOR is fatal — the caller wrote an unreachable
# locator. Total to-enshrine count is persisted so rbfd_enshrine can decide
# whether to submit Cloud Build at all.
zrbfd_enshrine_classify_slots() {
  zrbfd_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "zrbfd_enshrine_classify_slots: token required"

  buc_step "Classifying enshrine slots (skip-if-set vs. submit)"

  local z_n=""
  local z_origin_var=""
  local z_anchor_var=""
  local z_origin=""
  local z_anchor=""
  local z_pkg_path=""
  local z_tag=""
  local z_class_file=""
  local z_status_file=""
  local z_response_file=""
  local z_stderr_file=""
  local z_http_code=""
  local z_to_enshrine_count=0

  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ORIGIN"
    z_anchor_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ANCHOR"
    z_origin="${!z_origin_var:-}"
    z_anchor="${!z_anchor_var:-}"
    z_class_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}${z_n}_classification.txt"

    if test -n "${z_anchor}"; then
      case "${z_anchor}" in
        *:*) : ;;
        *)   buc_die "Invalid ${z_anchor_var} locator format (expected package-path:tag): ${z_anchor}" ;;
      esac
      z_pkg_path="${z_anchor%:*}"
      z_tag="${z_anchor##*:}"
      test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
      test -n "${z_tag}"      || buc_die "Tag is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"

      z_status_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}${z_n}_skip_status.txt"
      z_response_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}${z_n}_skip_response.txt"
      z_stderr_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}${z_n}_skip_stderr.txt"

      curl --head -sS \
        --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
        --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}" \
        -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}" \
        -H "Accept: ${ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES}" \
        -w "%{http_code}" \
        -o "${z_response_file}" \
        "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_pkg_path}/manifests/${z_tag}" \
        > "${z_status_file}" 2>"${z_stderr_file}" \
        || buc_die "HEAD request failed for pre-populated ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor} — see ${z_stderr_file}"

      z_http_code=$(<"${z_status_file}")
      test -n "${z_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty for pre-populated ${z_anchor_var}"

      if test "${z_http_code}" = "404"; then
        buc_die "Pre-populated ${z_anchor_var} not reachable in GAR: ${z_anchor} — caller wrote an unresolvable locator"
      elif test "${z_http_code}" != "200"; then
        buc_die "Unexpected HTTP ${z_http_code} for pre-populated ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
      fi

      printf 'skip' > "${z_class_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write classification for slot ${z_n}"
      buc_info "Slot ${z_n} pre-populated and reachable: ${z_anchor}"
    elif test -n "${z_origin}"; then
      printf 'enshrine' > "${z_class_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write classification for slot ${z_n}"
      z_to_enshrine_count=$((z_to_enshrine_count + 1))
      buc_log_args "Slot ${z_n} to enshrine: ORIGIN=${z_origin}"
    else
      printf 'empty' > "${z_class_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write classification for slot ${z_n}"
    fi
  done

  printf '%s' "${z_to_enshrine_count}" > "${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}to_enshrine_count.txt" \
    || buc_die "Failed to persist to-enshrine count"
}

# Internal: Submit enshrine Cloud Build job
input.new_string: # Internal: Submit enshrine Cloud Build job

input.old_string:
# ORIGIN substitution per slot: pass through only when the slot is classified
  # "enshrine"; "skip" (pre-populated ANCHOR) and "empty" map to empty string so
  # the Cloud Build step omits them.
  local z_sub_n=""
  local z_sub_class_file=""
  local z_sub_class=""
  local z_sub_origin_var=""
  local z_sub_origin_1=""
  local z_sub_origin_2=""
  local z_sub_origin_3=""
  for z_sub_n in 1 2 3; do
    z_sub_class_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}${z_sub_n}_classification.txt"
    z_sub_class=$(<"${z_sub_class_file}")
    z_sub_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_sub_n}_ORIGIN"
    if test "${z_sub_class}" = "enshrine"; then
      case "${z_sub_n}" in
        1) z_sub_origin_1="${!z_sub_origin_var:-}" ;;
        2) z_sub_origin_2="${!z_sub_origin_var:-}" ;;
        3) z_sub_origin_3="${!z_sub_origin_var:-}" ;;
      esac
    fi
  done
input.new_string:
# ORIGIN substitution per slot: pass non-empty ORIGIN through verbatim; empty
  # slots become empty substitutions so the CB step's `test -n "${ORIGIN}"
  # || continue` guard skips them.
  local -r z_sub_origin_1="${RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN:-}"
  local -r z_sub_origin_2="${RBRV_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN:-}"
  local -r z_sub_origin_3="${RBRV_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN:-}"

content.text: Notching the simplification.

params.intent: enshrine simplify: remove BBAA0 classifier overload, drop orchestration's enshrine-airgap-validate call. The classifier conflated two qualitatively different jobs into a single tabtarget: (1) mirror upstream + write back ANCHOR (forge case), (2) HEAD-validate orchestration-pinned hallmark (airgap-bottle case). Refining the switch was wrong-direction; the right move was to remove the overload so each job has a clean home. The HEAD-200 'skip' path was framed as an optimization but actively breaks enshrine's contract: HEAD-200 on a cached locator proves only that some manifest exists at that path, not that it matches current upstream. Skipping based on cache reachability silently locks the vessel to a stale digest, hiding the upstream-rotated case (the case Brad's earlier commit 14242011 records empirically when rust:slim-bookworm digest moved 5ae2d2ef98 to caaf9ca7ac). rbfd_enshrine now: (a) requires at least one slot has RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN populated (ANCHOR-only is no longer a valid input shape; ANCHOR is a derived artifact of this operation, not a pin); (b) unconditionally submits the Cloud Build job that inspects current upstream, computes the content-addressed locator from the live manifest digest, and copies into GAR; (c) extract overwrites whatever was previously committed in RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR. Re-runs are idempotent w.r.t. upstream state, not w.r.t. the file's prior contents — that distinction was the original design mistake. Internal pipeline: zrbfd_enshrine_classify_slots removed entirely (84 lines), zrbfd_enshrine_submit's classification-file read loop replaced with direct ${RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN:-} reads. Empty ORIGIN slots become empty CB substitutions; the CB step's existing `test -n ${ORIGIN} || continue` guard skips them naturally. Orchestration: rbtdro_onboarding.rs drops the enshrine-airgap-validate call after the airgap-bottle anchor write; the forge hallmark's existence in GAR is established by ordain-forge's success above, no separate validation step needed. airgap_sigil local removed (now unused). Block comment + docstring updated to remove skip-if-set framing. Commit-message string in source: removed coronet prefix from new commit string per directive (coronets should not appear in source). Pre-existing coronet-prefixed commit strings elsewhere in this file (BBAA0/BBAAu) are left as-is; broader sweep is follow-up work. Spec/handbook drift remains: RBSAE-ark_enshrine.adoc still describes skip-if-set classifier; RBS0-SpecTop.adoc rbrv_image_origin/rbrv_image_anchor linked-term descriptions still describe skip-if-set semantics; rbev-bottle-ifrit-airgap/rbrv.env head comment still says 'enshrine HEAD-validates'. These are documentation drift, deferred to a follow-up notch per operator pause. BCG audit on edited bash: test command over [[ ]], braced quoted expansion, || buc_die on every I/O, one declaration per line, mutable locals for synthesized values (Exception 2), no bash 4+ features, no heredocs, no inline shellcheck. Verification: bash -n green on rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh; theurge build green (rbtdro_onboarding.rs compiles); empirical re-run of ordain-airgap deferred per docket — operator drives next mount. This notch parallels f365fe89's revert (03a2a17a) — net effect from BBAA0 baseline: BBAA0's enshrine-overload pattern reverted, orchestration's pin-validation step removed, spec/comments drift TBD.

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_ark_enshrine}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation enshrines upstream base images into
5	{gar_service}
6	as content-addressed, human-readable anchors.
7	The
8	{rbtr_director}
9	submits a Cloud Build job that copies each declared
10	{rbrv_image_origin}
11	from its upstream registry to GAR using
12	the reliquary skopeo image
13	(`skopeo copy --all`), preserving the manifest list and all per-platform manifests for multi-platform images.
14	The locator results are returned via `buildStepOutputs` and
15	{rbrv_image_anchor}
16	is then written back to the vessel regime locally as a
17	{rbst_locator}.
18	
19	NOTE: Slots whose
20	{rbrv_image_anchor}
21	is already populated are processed under skip-if-set semantics.
22	The Director HEAD-validates that the locator resolves in
23	{gar_service}
24	and leaves the slot's
25	{rbrv_image_anchor}
26	untouched — no Cloud Build copy occurs for that slot.
27	This admits hallmark-base vessels: a vessel whose base image is an internal hallmark (e.g., the airgap-bottle building FROM the just-ordained forge) carries an
28	{rbrv_image_anchor}
29	of `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/«BASENAME»:«HALLMARK»` written by the orchestrating procedure (handbook step or onboarding-sequence fixture) before enshrine runs; enshrine then verifies the hallmark is reachable rather than re-mirroring it.
30	A vessel whose every slot is pre-populated requires no Cloud Build submission — enshrine reduces to local HEAD validation.
31	
32	NOTE: Enshrine is a pre-build preparation step.
33	It must be run before
34	{rbtgo_ark_conjure}
35	when anchored base images are desired.
36	Conjure works without anchors (passing ORIGIN through), but anchored images provide reproducibility and air-gap readiness.
37	
38	NOTE: Enshrine runs on Cloud Build, not the Director's workstation.
39	No local skopeo installation is required.
40	The GCB step uses Mason SA ambient credentials via the metadata server for GAR writes.
41	
42	The enshrine operation:
43	
44	//axhopt_typed_parameter
45	{rbrv_sigil}
46	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
47	.. {rbbc_require}
48	vessel provided as first argument
49	.. {rbbc_require}
50	vessel declares at least one base-image slot — that is, at least one of slots 1-3 has either
51	{rbrv_image_origin}
52	or
53	{rbrv_image_anchor}
54	populated
55	.. {rbbc_require}
56	vessel mode is `conjure` (`RBRV_VESSEL_MODE`)
57	.. {rbbc_require}
58	reliquary tool images exist in GAR (HEAD check on `docker:latest` canary)
59	
60	//axhos_step
61	. *Classify Slots*:
62	.. For each indexed slot (n = 1, 2, 3):
63	... If
64	{rbrv_image_anchor}
65	is set: classify as *pre-populated*. HEAD-request the locator's `«PACKAGE-PATH»:«TAG»` manifest in
66	{gar_service}
67	. {rbbc_require}
68	HTTP 200 — on 404, fail with directive naming the missing locator (caller wrote an unreachable
69	{rbrv_image_anchor}
70	). The slot's
71	{rbrv_image_anchor}
72	is left intact; no Cloud Build copy is performed.
73	... Otherwise, if
74	{rbrv_image_origin}
75	is set: classify as *to-enshrine*. ORIGIN is passed to the Cloud Build step for inspect-and-copy.
76	... Otherwise the slot is empty and is omitted from the build.
77	.. If every declared slot is pre-populated, skip the Cloud Build submission entirely — the operation reduces to local HEAD validation.
78	
79	//axhos_step
80	. *Submit Enshrine Cloud Build Job* (only if any slot is *to-enshrine*):
81	.. Compose `builds.create` JSON with:
82	... Single step using
83	the reliquary skopeo image
84	as the builder image (script: `rbgje01-enshrine-copy.sh`)
85	... `_RBGE_*` substitutions: `_RBGE_GAR_HOST`, `_RBGE_GAR_PATH`, `_RBGE_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN` (n = 1, 2, 3; empty for unused slots). Base images are stored in the shared `{gar_enshrines_namespace}/` namespace, with each anchor as a peer package (`{gar_enshrines_namespace}/«ANCHOR»:«ANCHOR»`), not per-vessel.
86	... Mason SA, private pool, timeout
87	.. Submit via `builds.create` and wait for completion
88	.. The GCB step, for each non-empty ORIGIN slot:
89	... Fetches the raw upstream manifest (`skopeo inspect --raw`) and computes its sha256 digest (`sha256sum` of the raw manifest bytes)
90	... {rbbc_store}
91	`<<DIGEST>>` -- the sha256 digest of the upstream manifest (manifest list digest for multi-platform images, single manifest digest for single-platform images)
92	... Constructs anchor: sanitize full origin string (`:` `/` replaced with `-`), append first 10 hex chars of sha256 digest
93	... Example: `python:3.11-slim` with `sha256:abc123def4...` yields anchor `python-3.11-slim-abc123def4`
94	... Composes the
95	{rbst_locator}
96	as `{gar_enshrines_namespace}/«ANCHOR»:«ANCHOR»` (e.g., `{gar_enshrines_namespace}/python-3.11-slim-abc123def4:python-3.11-slim-abc123def4`)
97	... Copies upstream image into the shared `{gar_enshrines_namespace}/` namespace in GAR as a peer package at `{gar_enshrines_namespace}/«ANCHOR»:«ANCHOR»` using `skopeo copy --all`, transferring the manifest list and all per-platform manifests atomically. Each anchor is its own package under the namespace — the path segment carries the anchor identity, and the tag redundantly restates it so divergence is a visible bug. The anchor is vessel-independent — multiple vessels sharing the same base image origin share one enshrined package.
98	... {rbbc_require}
99	copy succeeds -- the locator now resolves to a permanent, content-addressed reference in
100	{gar_service}
101	containing all upstream platforms
102	... Single-platform upstream images are handled identically -- `skopeo copy --all` is a no-op on the platform dimension when only one platform exists
103	.. Writes JSON locator results to `/builder/outputs/output` (returned as `buildStepOutputs`); each slot's `anchor` field carries the full locator string
104	
105	//axhos_step
106	. *Extract Locators and Write Regime* (only for *to-enshrine* slots):
107	.. Read `buildStepOutputs[0]` from the completed build result (base64-decoded JSON)
108	.. For each slot present in the result:
109	... Write locator string to `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR` in the vessel's `rbrv.env`
110	.. *Pre-populated* slots are not touched — their existing
111	{rbrv_image_anchor}
112	values stand
113	
114	//axhoot_typed_output
115	{rbrv_image_anchor}
116	Per-slot locator string written to the vessel regime.
117	
118	//axhoc_completion
119	Successful execution produces anchored base image references in
120	{gar_service}
121	(with all upstream platforms preserved) and updates the vessel regime with
122	{rbrv_image_anchor}
123	values.
124	The vessel is now ready for reproducible
125	{rbtgo_ark_conjure}
126	builds using GAR-mirrored base images.
127	Multi-platform conjure vessels pull the correct platform from the anchored manifest list automatically.
128	Re-running enshrine with the same
129	{rbrv_image_origin}
130	but a different upstream digest produces a new locator (upstream image was updated).

44	success, not by a separate validation step here.
45	
46	NOTE: Enshrine is a pre-build preparation step.
47	It must be run before
48	{rbtgo_ark_conjure}
49	when anchored base images are desired.
50	Conjure works without anchors (passing ORIGIN through), but anchored images provide reproducibility and air-gap readiness.
51	
52	NOTE: Enshrine runs on Cloud Build, not the Director's workstation.
53	No local skopeo installation is required.
54	The GCB step uses Mason SA ambient credentials via the metadata server for GAR writes.
55	
56	The enshrine operation:
57	
58	//axhopt_typed_parameter
59	{rbrv_sigil}
60	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
61	.. {rbbc_require}
62	vessel provided as first argument
63	.. {rbbc_require}
64	vessel declares at least one upstream base-image slot — that is, at least one of slots 1-3 has
65	{rbrv_image_origin}
66	populated.
67	{rbrv_image_anchor}
68	is computed by this operation; an ANCHOR-only slot is not a valid input shape
69	.. {rbbc_require}
70	vessel mode is `conjure` (`RBRV_VESSEL_MODE`)
71	.. {rbbc_require}
72	reliquary tool images exist in GAR (HEAD check on `docker:latest` canary)
73	
74	//axhos_step
75	. *Submit Enshrine Cloud Build Job*:
76	.. Compose `builds.create` JSON with:
77	... Single step using
78	the reliquary skopeo image
79	as the builder image (script: `rbgje01-enshrine-copy.sh`)
80	... `_RBGE_*` substitutions: `_RBGE_GAR_HOST`, `_RBGE_GAR_PATH`, `_RBGE_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN` (n = 1, 2, 3; empty for unused slots). Base images are stored in the shared `{gar_enshrines_namespace}/` namespace, with each anchor as a peer package (`{gar_enshrines_namespace}/«ANCHOR»:«ANCHOR»`), not per-vessel.
81	... Mason SA, private pool, timeout
82	.. Submit via `builds.create` and wait for completion
83	.. The GCB step, for each non-empty ORIGIN slot:
84	... Fetches the raw upstream manifest (`skopeo inspect --raw`) and computes its sha256 digest (`sha256sum` of the raw manifest bytes)
85	... {rbbc_store}
86	`<<DIGEST>>` -- the sha256 digest of the upstream manifest (manifest list digest for multi-platform images, single manifest digest for single-platform images)
87	... Constructs anchor: sanitize full origin string (`:` `/` replaced with `-`), append first 10 hex chars of sha256 digest
88	... Example: `python:3.11-slim` with `sha256:abc123def4...` yields anchor `python-3.11-slim-abc123def4`
89	... Composes the
90	{rbst_locator}
91	as `{gar_enshrines_namespace}/«ANCHOR»:«ANCHOR»` (e.g., `{gar_enshrines_namespace}/python-3.11-slim-abc123def4:python-3.11-slim-abc123def4`)
92	... Copies upstream image into the shared `{gar_enshrines_namespace}/` namespace in GAR as a peer package at `{gar_enshrines_namespace}/«ANCHOR»:«ANCHOR»` using `skopeo copy --all`, transferring the manifest list and all per-platform manifests atomically. Each anchor is its own package under the namespace — the path segment carries the anchor identity, and the tag redundantly restates it so divergence is a visible bug. The anchor is vessel-independent — multiple vessels sharing the same base image origin share one enshrined package.
93	... {rbbc_require}
94	copy succeeds -- the locator now resolves to a permanent, content-addressed reference in
95	{gar_service}
96	containing all upstream platforms
97	... Single-platform upstream images are handled identically -- `skopeo copy --all` is a no-op on the platform dimension when only one platform exists
98	.. Writes JSON locator results to `/builder/outputs/output` (returned as `buildStepOutputs`); each slot's `anchor` field carries the full locator string
99	
100	//axhos_step

file.content:
[[rbrv_image_origin]]
//axvr_variable axd_optional rbst_image_ref
{rbrv_image_origin}::
Base image declaration for
{rbrv_regime}
conjuring vessels.
Two complementary roles depending on whether the slot's
{rbrv_image_anchor}
is set:
+
- *When the slot is open (no ANCHOR yet)*: ORIGIN is the upstream registry tag the vessel author intends to mirror (e.g., `python:3.11-slim`). {rbtgo_ark_enshrine} consumes it as the source for `skopeo inspect`/`copy`.
- *When the slot is pre-populated with a hallmark-base ANCHOR*: ORIGIN is informational — a lineage marker (typically the producing vessel's sigil, e.g., `rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge`). {rbtgo_ark_enshrine} ignores ORIGIN under skip-if-set; conjure resolves the base from
{rbrv_image_anchor}.
+
The Dockerfile uses `ARG RBF_IMAGE_n` / `FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_n}` -- the Foundry substitutes the resolved reference via `--build-arg` at conjure time.

[[rbrv_image_anchor]]
//axvr_variable axd_optional rbst_locator
{rbrv_image_anchor}::
{rbst_locator}
naming a GAR-resident base image for
{rbrv_regime}
conjuring vessels.
The locator's namespace identifies how the slot was populated:
+
- `{gar_enshrines_namespace}/«ANCHOR»:«ANCHOR»` — written by
{rbtgo_ark_enshrine}.
The anchor itself is `«sanitized-origin»-«10-char-sha256»` (e.g., `python-3.11-slim-abc123def4`); the locator embeds it as both path segment and tag.
Enshrined images are stored as peer packages under the shared `{gar_enshrines_namespace}/` namespace, not per-vessel — vessels sharing the same base image origin share one enshrined artifact.
- `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/«BASENAME»:«HALLMARK»` — written by an orchestrating procedure (handbook step or onboarding-sequence fixture) when the vessel's base image is an internal hallmark (e.g., the airgap-bottle building FROM the just-ordained forge image).
The locator points at the specific
{rbtga_ark_image}
inside the producer hallmark's subtree.
{rbtgo_ark_enshrine}
treats such pre-populated slots under skip-if-set semantics — HEAD-validates and leaves the slot intact.
+
When present, conjure resolves the base image from GAR via this locator (parsing path and tag, prepending {rbrr_cloud_prefix} at use-site per the {rbst_locator} convention).
When absent, conjure passes
{rbrv_image_origin}
through as the upstream tag (development path).

[[rbrv_group_grafting]]
//axvr_group axd_conditional
{rbrv_group_grafting}::

#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Regime Vessel - Bottle Ifrit (Airgapped Conjure)

# Core Identity
RBRV_SIGIL=rbev-bottle-ifrit-airgap
RBRV_DESCRIPTION="Rust ifrit attack binary with network tools and echo service (airgapped)"
RBRV_USER=ifrit

# Vessel Mode
RBRV_VESSEL_MODE=conjure

# Egress Mode
RBRV_EGRESS_MODE=airgap

# Tool Image Reliquary
RBRV_RELIQUARY=r260430070211

# Conjuring Configuration — airgap Dockerfile in shared context, builds FROM enshrined forge
RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/common-ifrit-context/Dockerfile.airgap"
RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/common-ifrit-context"
RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS="linux/amd64 linux/arm64"

# Base Images (ARG/FROM pattern — IMAGE_1 is the forge image inside its hallmark subtree)
# ORIGIN names the producer vessel (lineage); ANCHOR carries the locator
# pointing at the just-ordained forge image:
#   rbi_hm/<FORGE_HALLMARK>/image:<FORGE_HALLMARK>
# Operator (handbook) or orchestration (onboarding-sequence) writes ANCHOR
# after ordaining the forge. Enshrine then HEAD-validates it (skip-if-set).
RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN=rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge
RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR=

# eof

file.content:
buh_line "for it inside your ${RBYC_DEPOT}. Run ${RBYC_ENSHRINE} on the forge:"
  buh_e
  buh_tt "   " "${RBZ_ENSHRINE_VESSEL}" "" " ${z_forge_vessel}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "${RBYC_ENSHRINE} resolves the upstream base for the forge ${RBYC_VESSEL}"
  buh_line "and writes the locator back into its ${RBYC_RBRV}. Slots whose ANCHOR"
  buh_line "is already populated are HEAD-validated against your ${RBYC_DEPOT} and"
  buh_line "left untouched — re-running is idempotent."
  buh_e

  buh_step1 "${RBYC_CONJURE} the forge ${RBYC_TETHERED}, then point the airgap ${RBYC_BOTTLE} at it"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The forge is a project-authored toolchain image. It pre-stages"
  buh_line "apt packages and warms the cargo cache so the airgap build"
  buh_line "downstream has nothing to fetch."
  buh_e
  buh_line "Build it on the ${RBYC_TETHERED} pool — the forge ${RBYC_RBRV} declares:"
  buh_e
  buh_code "   RBRV_EGRESS_MODE=tether"
  buh_e
  buh_line "${RBYC_ORDAIN} reads this and routes to the ${RBYC_TETHERED} pool:"
  buh_e
  buh_tt "   " "${RBZ_ORDAIN_HALLMARK}" "" " ${z_forge_vessel}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "Wall-clock ~15-20 minutes across the declared platforms. The"
  buh_line "forge is toolchain plumbing that your customer code will be"
  buh_line "built against — it is not itself customer code, which is why"
  buh_line "${RBYC_TETHERED} build is acceptable at this layer."
  buh_e
  buh_line "Now bridge: the airgap ${RBYC_VESSEL} ${z_lk_airgap} declares the"
  buh_line "forge as its base:"
  buh_e
  buh_code "   RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN=rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge"
  buh_code "   RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR="
  buh_e
  buh_line "ORIGIN names the producer ${RBYC_VESSEL} (lineage); ANCHOR will hold"
  buh_line "the locator pointing at the forge's just-built ${RBYC_HALLMARK} inside"
  buh_line "your ${RBYC_DEPOT}'s hallmark namespace. ${RBYC_ORDAIN} wrote the forge"
  buh_line "${RBYC_HALLMARK} to the fact file — capture it:"
  buh_e
  buh_code "   export FORGE_HALLMARK=\$(cat ${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK})"
  buh_e
  buh_line "Open the airgap ${RBYC_RBRV} and set ANCHOR to the forge image's locator:"
  buh_e
  buh_code "   RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR=rbi_hm/\${FORGE_HALLMARK}/image:\${FORGE_HALLMARK}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "Substitute the captured hallmark into the locator. Commit the change."
  buh_e
  buh_line "Now run ${RBYC_ENSHRINE} on the airgap ${RBYC_VESSEL} as a validation step —"
  buh_line "${RBYC_ENSHRINE} sees the populated ANCHOR, HEAD-validates that the forge"
  buh_line "${RBYC_HALLMARK} is reachable in your ${RBYC_DEPOT}, and leaves the slot"
  buh_line "untouched. No copy, no Cloud Build job — just a validation gate."
  buh_e
  buh_tt "   " "${RBZ_ENSHRINE_VESSEL}" "" " ${z_airgap_vessel}"
  buh_e
  if test "${z_airgap_base_enshrined}" = "1"; then
    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_YES}Airgap base anchor populated — forge is ready to serve as airgap base"
  else
    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_NO}Airgap base anchor empty — ${RBYC_CONJURE} the forge, then write the locator into the airgap ${RBYC_RBRV}"
  fi

input.old_string:
buh_line "${RBYC_ENSHRINE} resolves the upstream base for the forge ${RBYC_VESSEL}"
  buh_line "and writes the locator back into its ${RBYC_RBRV}. Slots whose ANCHOR"
  buh_line "is already populated are HEAD-validated against your ${RBYC_DEPOT} and"
  buh_line "left untouched — re-running is idempotent."
input.new_string:
buh_line "${RBYC_ENSHRINE} inspects the current upstream image, computes a"
  buh_line "content-addressed locator from the live manifest digest, copies the"
  buh_line "image into your ${RBYC_DEPOT}, and writes the locator back into the"
  buh_line "vessel's ${RBYC_RBRV}, overwriting any prior value. Re-running mirrors"
  buh_line "current upstream — if upstream rotates, the next call writes a"
  buh_line "different locator and that is the operation surfacing the change."

  buh_e
  buh_line "An airgap supply chain has three links. Any future airgap build"
  buh_line "follows the same shape:"
  buh_e
  buh_line "   1. ${RBYC_ENSHRINE} the upstream base into your ${RBYC_DEPOT}"
  buh_line "   2. ${RBYC_CONJURE} the forge ${RBYC_TETHERED}, re-${RBYC_ENSHRINE} its ${RBYC_HALLMARK}"
  buh_line "   3. ${RBYC_CONJURE} the final ${RBYC_BOTTLE} ${RBYC_AIRGAP} from the enshrined forge"

case "${z_line}" in RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER=?*) z_has_depot=1; break ;; esac
    done < "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"
  fi

  local z_airgap_base_enshrined=0
  if test -f "${z_airgap_rbrv}"; then
    local z_anchor=""
    z_anchor=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${z_airgap_rbrv}" "RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR") || z_anchor=""
    test -n "${z_anchor}" && z_airgap_base_enshrined=1
  fi

  local z_airgap_ordained=0
  if test -f "${z_moriah_rbrn}"; then
    local z_moriah_hallmark=""
    z_moriah_hallmark=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${z_moriah_rbrn}" "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK") || z_moriah_hallmark=""
