/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/Tools/apck/APCPS-PrototypeSpecification.md:114:**Purpose.** Preserve verbatim real-world clipboard contents from every focus with changed content so the fixture library can grow from real traffic, so Epic's actual flavor structure can be studied, and so a classifier misfire still leaves an artifact on disk to post-mortem.
/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/Tools/apck/APCPS-PrototypeSpecification.md:116:**Trigger.** Every focus with changed clipboard content — executed after the classifier runs so the verdict can be tagged into the filename, but before any Clinical-branch clipboard zero-out. The same-as-last short-circuit at the top of the focus handler still applies: re-focusing with unchanged content does not re-harvest.
/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/Tools/apck/APCPS-PrototypeSpecification.md:120:**Naming — raw harvest.** Raw captures are written as `{N}-in.{tag}.{ext}` where `{tag}` is `clinical` or `nonclinical` — the classifier's verdict at the moment of capture. `N` seeds at 10000 for an empty directory, otherwise `max_leading_digit_run + 1` across every filename whose name begins with digits. Files sharing the same `N` group all artifacts of one capture — e.g., `10000-in.clinical.rtf`, `10000-in.clinical.utf8.txt`, `10000-in.clinical.utf16.txt`, `10000-in.txt`, `10000-out.txt`, and `10000.json` are six artifacts of clinical capture 10000. Gaps in the numeric sequence are not filled — the scan advances past the current maximum. Filenames that do not begin with a digit are ignored in the max calculation, so `apcap.log`, `container-log.txt`, and any user-placed `README` or `notes` co-exist without perturbing indexing.
/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/Tools/apck/APCPS-PrototypeSpecification.md:141:arboard remains on the classifier's `get_text()` path. The separation is load-bearing: arboard is a simple, stable, cross-platform read of the UTF-8 flavor used to feed the clinical-gate heuristic; `apcrb_pasteboard` is lower-level, Mac-only, riskier FFI code and owns enumeration + multi-flavor capture for disk. A break in `apcrb` (FFI error, empty pasteboard, non-macOS build) surfaces as an absent `{N}-in.{tag}.*` artifact and an `apcrl_error_now!` line in the log — it does not cascade into the classifier.

params.intent: spec/comment drift cleanup after enshrine simplification: align RBSAE-ark_enshrine.adoc, RBS0-SpecTop.adoc rbrv_image_origin/rbrv_image_anchor linked-term descriptions, rbhoda_director_airgap handbook prose, and the airgap-bottle rbrv.env head comment with the simplified bash. RBSAE: replace 'skip-if-set semantics + Director HEAD-validates' head NOTE with 'pure mirror — every call inspects current upstream, computes the locator from live manifest digest, copies to GAR, overwrites RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR'; add adjacent NOTE that hallmark-pin slots (airgap-bottle) do not invoke this operation, with hallmark existence established by producer's ordain success; Validate Input requires ORIGIN populated (ANCHOR-only is no longer a valid input shape since ANCHOR is a derived value); Classify Slots step removed entirely; Submit step header drops 'only if any slot is to-enshrine' qualifier (always submitted); Extract step drops 'only for to-enshrine slots' qualifier and 'pre-populated slots not touched' clause, replaced with 'overwriting any prior value'. RBS0 [[rbrv_image_origin]]: replace open-vs-pre-populated framing with upstream-base-vs-hallmark-base — upstream-base says enshrine overwrites ANCHOR every call, hallmark-base says enshrine is not invoked. RBS0 [[rbrv_image_anchor]]: same simplification on the namespace-keyed bullets — rbi_es written/overwritten by enshrine as derived value of current upstream, rbi_hm written by orchestration with no enshrine invocation. rbhoda_director_airgap step 1 prose: drop 'slots whose ANCHOR is already populated are HEAD-validated and left untouched' framing, replace with 'inspects current upstream, computes locator from live manifest digest, overwrites RBRV ANCHOR — re-running mirrors current upstream and surfaces upstream rotation as a different locator'. Step 2 prose: drop 'run enshrine on the airgap vessel as a validation step' paragraph entirely, replace with 'enshrine is not invoked on the airgap vessel — the hallmark's existence in your DEPOT is established by the forge's ordain success above; conjure resolves the locator at airgap-bottle build time'. Step 'pattern' summary: link 2 reworded from 're-enshrine its hallmark' to 'write its hallmark locator into the consumer's RBRV ANCHOR'. rbev-bottle-ifrit-airgap/rbrv.env head comment: drop 'Enshrine then HEAD-validates it (skip-if-set)', replace with 'Conjure resolves it at airgap-bottle build time; enshrine is not invoked on this vessel'. Other touch-checks: zrbfd_registry_preflight HEAD check (rbfd:341) is the ordain-side preflight and stays untouched per design — different purpose (fast-fail before launching conjure CB if enshrined image missing); RCG/AXLA/APCK 'classifier' references are unrelated naming-convention or PHI-clipboard contexts. BCG audit on edited bash file (rbhoda_director_airgap.sh): no test-vs-bracket changes, no new variable patterns, only buh_line / buh_code / buh_e prose changes; bash -n green. AsciiDoc edits preserve linked-term attribute references and structure (NOTE blocks, step bullets). Verification: bash -n green on rbhoda_director_airgap.sh and rbev-bottle-ifrit-airgap/rbrv.env. Empirical retry of ordain-airgap is running in background (tt/rbtd-s.FixtureCase.onboarding-sequence.sh rbtdro_onboarding_ordain_airgap).

content.text:
No — `93787859` is Rust-only. One file touched, 9 string-literal edits in `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdro_onboarding.rs` (the orchestration source), no bash files.

The previous notch `a446ab8f` did touch one bash file (`rbhoda_director_airgap.sh` — the handbook prose), but that's not in the running fixture's path.

**Concretely for the running fixture:** the theurge binary was built and launched at 09:39 from the binary at HEAD then; the binary won't pick up Rust source edits made after that. The bash tabtargets it shells out to (`rbw-dE`, `rbw-fO`, etc.) re-source on each invocation, so bash changes WOULD take effect mid-run — but the fixture's path through bash hits `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh` (committed in `4e7b5f29` before fixture launch) and never touches the handbook script `rbhoda_director_airgap.sh`. So nothing in the post-launch notches alters fixture behavior.

content.text:
## Progress

Two of three CB stages are done. Currently in the third (the airgap-bottle conjure, the long one).

| Step | Status | Wall clock |
|---|---|---|
| 1. Enshrine forge (rust:slim-bookworm → GAR) | ✅ SUCCESS | 18s |
| 2. Ordain forge (conjure tethered) | ✅ SUCCESS | hallmark `c260430094037-r260430164041`, vouch +1m31s |
| 3. Write airgap-bottle ANCHOR (orchestration) | ✅ done | instant |
| 4. Ordain airgap (conjure airgap) | 🔄 WORKING (poll 27/960, ~2 min in) | in progress |

**Confirmation that the simplification works as intended:** the enshrine step submitted the CB job unconditionally (no classifier, no skip-if-set), copied rust:slim-bookworm to GAR, and wrote the locator back. The recomputed locator was `rbi_es/rust-slim-bookworm-caaf9ca7ac:rust-slim-bookworm-caaf9ca7ac` — same as the previously-committed value, because upstream `rust:slim-bookworm` hasn't rotated since the prior enshrine. Idempotent w.r.t. upstream state, exactly as the new contract describes.

The airgap conjure typically takes ~15-20 minutes for the full Rust compile + multi-platform ifrit build + vouch. Then drive hallmark to moriah → final commit → PASS. ETA: ~15-25 min.

a446ab8f jjb:1011-4e7b5f29:₢BBAAs:n: spec/comment drift cleanup after enshrine simplification: align RBSAE-ark_enshrine.adoc, RBS0-SpecTop.adoc rbrv_image_origin/rbrv_image_anchor linked-term descriptions, rbhoda_director_airgap handbook prose, and the airgap-bottle rbrv.env head comment with the simplified bash. RBSAE: replace 'skip-if-set semantics + Director HEAD-validates' head NOTE with 'pure mirror — every call inspects current upstream, computes the locator from live manifest digest, copies to GAR, overwrites RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR'; add adjacent NOTE that hallmark-pin slots (airgap-bottle) do not invoke this operation, with hallmark existence established by producer's ordain success; Validate Input requires ORIGIN populated (ANCHOR-only is no longer a valid input shape since ANCHOR is a derived value); Classify Slots step removed entirely; Submit step header drops 'only if any slot is to-enshrine' qualifier (always submitted); Extract step drops 'only for to-enshrine slots' qualifier and 'pre-populated slots not touched' clause, replaced with 'overwriting any prior value'. RBS0 [[rbrv_image_origin]]: replace open-vs-pre-populated framing with upstream-base-vs-hallmark-base — upstream-base says enshrine overwrites ANCHOR every call, hallmark-base says enshrine is not invoked. Step 2 prose: drop 'run enshrine on the airgap vessel as a validation step' paragraph entirely, replace with 'enshrine is not invoked on the airgap vessel — the hallmark's existence in your DEPOT is established by the forge's ordain success above; conjure resolves the locator at airgap-bottle build time'. Step 'pattern' summary: link 2 reworded from 're-enshrine its hallmark' to 'write its hallmark locator into the consumer's RBRV ANCHOR'. rbev-bottle-ifrit-airgap/rbrv.env head comment: drop 'Enshrine then HEAD-validates it (skip-if-set)', replace with 'Conjure resolves it at airgap-bottle build time; enshrine is not invoked on this vessel'. Other touch-checks: zrbfd_registry_preflight HEAD check (rbfd:341) is the ordain-side preflight and stays untouched per design — different purpose (fast-fail before launching conjure CB if enshrined image missing); RCG/AXLA/APCK 'classifier' references are unrelated naming-convention or PHI-clipboard contexts. BCG audit on edited bash file (rbhoda_director_airgap.sh): no test-vs-bracket changes, no new variable patterns, only buh_line / buh_code / buh_e prose changes; bash -n green. AsciiDoc edits preserve linked-term attribute references and structure (NOTE blocks, step bullets). Verification: bash -n green on rbhoda_director_airgap.sh and rbev-bottle-ifrit-airgap/rbrv.env. Empirical retry of ordain-airgap is running in background (tt/rbtd-s.FixtureCase.onboarding-sequence.sh rbtdro_onboarding_ordain_airgap).
4e7b5f29 jjb:1011-03a2a17a:₢BBAAs:n: enshrine simplify: remove BBAA0 classifier overload, drop orchestration's enshrine-airgap-validate call. The classifier conflated two qualitatively different jobs into a single tabtarget: (1) mirror upstream + write back ANCHOR (forge case), (2) HEAD-validate orchestration-pinned hallmark (airgap-bottle case). content.text:
## ✅ PASS — `rbtdro_onboarding_ordain_airgap`

**Wall clock:** 28 min (09:39 → 10:07). One case, all stages green.

| Step | Outcome |
|---|---|
| Enshrine forge (rust:slim-bookworm → GAR, **unconditional CB**) | SUCCESS — 18s |
| Ordain forge (conjure tethered) | SUCCESS — hallmark `c260430094037-r260430164041` |
| Write airgap-bottle ANCHOR via orchestration | done — `rbi_hm/c260430094037-…/image:…` |
| Ordain airgap (conjure airgap pool, no public network) | SUCCESS — separate hallmark |
| Drive hallmark to `moriah` nameplate + commit | done |

**The simplification empirically validates:**
- Enshrine ran the CB job unconditionally despite the committed-stale ANCHOR; recomputed locator matched (upstream stable since prior enshrine).
- No `enshrine-airgap-validate` call to the airgap-bottle — the forge's `ordain-airgap` success established the hallmark in GAR; conjure resolved it directly.
- The committed-stale-anchor problem the original BBAAs run hit is gone by construction.

## New commits the orchestration wrote during the run

```
6edfe10b BBAA0 ordain-airgap: airgap-bottle hallmark + propagate to moriah
2f149320 ordain-airgap: write forge-hallmark locator into airgap-bottle base anchor
```

`2f149320` reflects my coronet-free string (notch `4e7b5f29`, made before launch). `6edfe10b` still says "BBAA0" because the running theurge binary was compiled at 09:39 — *before* the coronet sweep at `93787859`. After any future rebuild, that string will be coronet-free too. Empirical confirmation that running binaries don't pick up post-launch source edits, exactly as I described earlier.

## Heat status

Pace `BBAAs` docket wrap criterion is **green tally on `onboarding-sequence`** — i.e., the *full* fixture, not just this case. Cases still un-verified after this win: `propagate-moriah`, `ordain-pluml`, `ordain-graft`. Their preconditions may now hold (moriah's bottle hallmark just got driven; pluml/graft are independent CB modes).

Three options:
1. **Run remaining cases individually** — `tt/rbtd-s.FixtureCase.onboarding-sequence.sh rbtdro_onboarding_propagate_moriah` etc., one at a time. Lowest risk; surfaces failures cleanly.
2. **Run full fixture from top** — fastest if everything works, but re-runs the already-passed cases.
3. **Pause and review** — let the two new commits + the simplification settle before more empirical work.

What's your call?
